### Post by Zetheroo on 2007-10-26
I have tried to get the Dual-Head working in Gutsy... but all that happens is my external LCD is black and the green power light blinks.
I have the ATI Mobility Radeon X1400

### Post by n888 on 2007-10-27
The dual head mode with ati open source driver is broken in Gutsy.

I managed to fix it by installing the feisty ati driver. 

sudo dpkg -i ./xserver-xorg-video-ati_6.6.3-2ubuntu6_i386.deb

Or you can use fglrx driver.

---

### Post by eshaggy82 on 2007-10-30
T42, ATI Radeon Mobility 7500, Dual-Head using xinerama, External LCDs connected to DVI/VGA ports on docking station. Just upgraded to Gutsy and my Dual-Head, xorg.conf settings and everything display related went to the toilet.

THE FIX:

1. restore your xorg.conf from backup from before upgrade to Gutsy (if you have it available. if not, you have to restore it by following xorg.conf dual head setup instructions for xinerama.)
2. remove/uninstall package xserver-xorg-video-ati version 1.6.7.195-1ubuntu2
3. download xserver-xorg-video-ati_6.6.3-2ubuntu6_i386.deb (Google it)
4. install xserver-xorg-video-ati_6.6.3-2ubuntu6_i386.deb
5. select the new 'ati' driver to be used in System>Administration>Screens and Graphics
6. FULL REBOOT (restarting X is not enough)

---

### Post by eshaggy82 on 2007-10-30
BTW, Thanks to n888 for listing the exact package name of the previous version of the driver used in Feisty. Thanks very much!

---
